questionHtml:
A χ2 test at the 5% significance level is carried out to determine if there is significant evidence of a difference in the quality of the food cooked for the three meals.

The critical value for this test is 9.488.

The hypotheses for this test are:

H0: The quality of the food and the type of meal are independent.
H1: The quality of the food and the type of meal are not independent.

Find the χ2 statistic.
markschemeHtml:
11.0 (11.0212…)          A2

 

Note: Award A1 for a final answer of 11 if no unrounded answer is seen.

 

[2 marks]

meta.title: Find the χ2 statistic.
breadcrumbLabels: SL 4.11—Expected, observed, hypotheses, chi squared, gof, t-test
